Title: Identification of an additional protein involved in mannan biosynthesis

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Proposed to promote galactomannan biosynthesis; identified as an additional protein involved in mannan biosynthesis based on endosperm-specific expression and homology to functional Arabidopsis MSR genes.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly and specifically expressed in seed endosperm at 25 and 32 days post-anthesis; not detected in embryo or young leaves.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly and specifically expressed in seed endosperm during seed development.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GT65R family protein; DUF246 / GDP-fucose protein O-fucosyltransferase-like domain (PF10250); predicted glycosyltransferase-like protein with N-terminal transmembrane domain.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: EST profiling; RT-PCR tissue expression analysis; GFP fusion co-localization with Golgi marker ST-mRFP in tobacco leaves; comparative homology with Arabidopsis knockout/complementation evidence.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TfMSR was identified from fenugreek endosperm EST profiling as one of the highly expressed candidate genes associated with galactomannan accumulation. 2. TfMSR expression is endosperm-specific, matching the tissue of galactomannan deposition. 3. TfMSR protein localizes to the Golgi, consistent with a role in wall polysaccharide biosynthesis. 4. Functional inference is supported by Arabidopsis homologs AtMSR1 and AtMSR2, whose mutation reduces mannan content and mannan synthase activity.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing seeds at 25 and 32 days post-anthesis.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TfMSR failed to complement the Arabidopsis msr1 msr2 double mutant when driven by 35S, likely because the transgene was expressed at very low levels. Authors propose MSR proteins should be classified as GT65R rather than putative O-FucT until biochemical activity is proven.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Deep EST profiling of fenugreek seed endosperm.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for engineering seed galactomannan accumulation, but direct in planta functional validation in fenugreek is lacking and biochemical activity remains unresolved.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes Arabidopsis stem glucomannan/mannan accumulation and mannan synthase activity; major contributor among MSR homologs.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in all tissues tested, with higher levels in seedling roots, flowers, siliques, and stems especially the top region; strong GUS in leaf primordia, young leaves, roots, vascular tissues, guard cells, trichomes, flowers, siliques, phloem, and interfascicular fiber cells.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed broadly; enriched in roots, flowers, siliques, young stem regions, guard cells, trichomes, veins, phloem and interfascicular fibers.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall polysaccharide contributing to plant cell wall structure.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Stem cell walls, notably xylem vessels and epidermal cells
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GT65R family protein; DUF246 / O-FucT-like conserved domain with N-terminal transmembrane domain.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA knockout mutants (msr1-1, msr1-2); monosaccharide composition analysis; PACE; immunofluorescence labeling with anti-mannan antibody; in vitro mannan synthase assays in microsomes; complementation with 35S:AtMSR1.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Loss of AtMSR1 reduced stem mannosyl content by about 40% and reduced glucomannan detected by PACE and immunolabeling. 2. Microsomal mannan synthase activity decreased by about 47% in msr1 mutants. 3. Double mutants with msr2 showed stronger reductions, indicating partial redundancy. 4. 35S-driven AtMSR1 restored mannosyl levels in the msr1 msr2 double mutant, confirming causal involvement.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious morphological phenotype in single or double mutants; no significant change in crystalline cellulose content.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Primary inflorescence stems from 6- to 7-week-old plants; standard growth conditions.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RT-PCR; qRT-PCR; GUS reporter analysis; prior microarray support cited for AtMSR1.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors infer AtMSR1 is not itself the mannan synthase enzyme but an additional Golgi protein needed for full mannan biosynthetic activity, possibly affecting priming, glycosylation, folding, stability or activity of CSLA-containing machinery.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to increase mannan/glucomannan accumulation in cell walls, potentially in combination with CSLA genes; tissue-specific expression and unresolved biochemical mechanism should be considered.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports full Arabidopsis mannan/glucomannan accumulation and mannan synthase activity, functioning redundantly with AtMSR1 but with weaker single-mutant effect.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in all tissues tested, with higher levels in seedling roots, flowers, siliques, and stems especially top stem; GUS strong in leaf primordia, young leaves, roots, vascular tissue and epidermis of roots, tips of young lateral roots, flowers, siliques and stem phloem.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Broadly expressed with enrichment in roots, flowers, siliques, young stem regions, phloem, pistils, socket cells around trichomes, and root epidermis/vascular tissues.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall polysaccharide contributing to plant cell wall structure.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Stem cell walls, notably xylem vessels and epidermal cells
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GT65R family protein; DUF246 / O-FucT-like conserved domain with N-terminal transmembrane domain.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA knockout mutants (msr2-1, msr2-2, msr2-3); double mutant analysis with msr1; monosaccharide composition analysis; PACE; immunolabeling; in vitro mannan synthase assays; complementation with 35S:AtMSR2.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Single msr2 mutants did not show significant reduction in stem mannosyl residues or mannan synthase activity. 2. Loss of AtMSR2 enhanced the msr1 phenotype in double mutants, demonstrating partial redundancy in mannan biosynthesis. 3. 35S-driven AtMSR2 partially or completely restored mannosyl levels in the msr1 msr2 double mutant depending on expression level. 4. AtMSR2 therefore plays a subsidiary but real role in maintaining full mannan biosynthetic activity.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious morphological phenotype in single or double mutants.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Primary inflorescence stems from 6- to 7-week-old plants; standard growth conditions.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AtMSR2 contribution appears weaker than AtMSR1 in stems, possibly due to different cell-specific expression patterns rather than absence of biochemical capability.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be stacked with AtMSR1 or CSLA genes to reinforce mannan production; by itself may have weaker effect in some tissues.
